steps.title: Match the contrast
steps.content: The passage says respondents named curtailments more often than efficiencies. Turning off lights is a curtailment, so the completion should contrast that 19.6% response with a lower-percentage efficiency action from the data.

steps.title: Use the paired efficiency
steps.content:
• Choice A shifts to efficient cars or hybrids rather than the light-related efficiency.
  • Choice B gives a thermostat action, which is not the efficiency counterpart to turning off lights.
  • Choice C gives a transportation action, not the needed light-related efficiency.
The remaining choice supplies efficient light bulbs at 3.6%, making the intended contrast. The answer is D.
